nodes.x: God standeth in the congregation of the mighty; he judgeth among the gods.
¶ Judges and officers shalt thou make thee in all thy gates, which the Lord thy God giveth thee, throughout thy tribes: and they shall judge the people with just judgment.
Let the Lord, the God of the spirits of all flesh, set a man over the congregation,
The Lord standeth up to plead, and standeth to judge the people.
And the heavens shall declare his righteousness: for God is judge himself. Selah.
| of the great God. I say let not the child be divided ; let it live; and all the congregation said amen.
How long will ye judge unjustly, and accept the persons of the wicked? Selah.
It is not good to accept the person of the wicked, to overthrow the righteous in judgment.

on the earth, and the wicked shall slay the wicked. The Latter-day Saints have spoken of the judgments of God, often and long — now the nations will sec they arc living in the hour of these judgments.
Lord, how long shall the wicked, how long shall the wicked triumph?
But, behold, the judgments of God will overtake the wicked; and it is by the wicked that the wicked are punished; for it is the wicked that stir up the hearts of the children of men unto bloodshed.
Defend the poor and fatherless: do justice to the afflicted and needy.
Deliver the poor and needy: rid them out of the hand of the wicked.

Do not think, brethren, by this, that we would advise or direct
    that the poor be neglected in the least; this is not the desire of
    our hearts; for we are mindful of the word of our Father, which
    informs us that in His bosom it is decreed that the poor and the
    meek of the earth shall possess it.
Open thy mouth, judge righteously, and plead the cause of the poor and needy.
Deliver the poor and needy: rid them out of the hand of the wicked.
They turn the needy out of the way: the poor of the earth hide themselves together.
Defend the poor and fatherless: do justice to the afflicted and needy.
For he shall deliver the needy when he crieth; the poor also, and him that hath no helper.
And they shall look to the poor and the needy, and administer to their relief that they shall not suffer; and send them forth to the place which I have commanded them;
They know not, neither will they understand; they walk on in darkness: all the foundations of the earth are out of course.

they do. Do they know anything of the things of God? No. Brother Taylor observed, “Go to the priests of the day, and they cannot inform you.” There is a good reason why—because they know nothing directly in regard to the things of the kingdom, and they are equally ignorant with regard to the design of their present existence, to say nothing of either their prior or future existence. They are a…
But they know not the thoughts of the Lord, neither understand they his counsel: for he shall gather them as the sheaves into the floor.
They have not known nor understood: for he hath shut their eyes, that they cannot see; and their hearts, that they cannot understand.
Now they understood not the words which they spake; for they knew not that the earth should pass away.
Woe unto them that seek deep to hide their counsel from the Lord, and their works are in the dark, and they say, Who seeth us? and who knoweth us?
I have said, Ye are gods; and all of you are children of the most High.

this subject was more fully portrayed, according to the views of the Latter-day Saints, than is generally expressed by religious people in the world, for I believe that all religious people claim to be, and term themselves, the children of God. It may not be amiss to investigate, for a little while, the reality of this title, and see if we can come to some kind of an understanding in regard to…
Ye are of God, little children, and have overcome them: because greater is he that is in you, than he that is in the world.
For ye are all the children of God by faith in Christ Jesus.
Who is like unto the Lord our God, who dwelleth on high,
For the Lord is a great God, and a great King above all gods.
But ye shall die like men, and fall like one of the princes.
Behold, a king shall reign in righteousness, and princes shall rule in judgment.
Instead of thy fathers shall be thy children, whom thou mayest make princes in all the earth.
And their king shall go into captivity, he and his princes together, saith the Lord.
And I will give children unto them to be their princes, and babes shall rule over them.
And I will give children to be their princes, and babes shall rule over them.
Arise, O God, judge the earth: for thou shalt inherit all nations.
O let the nations be glad and sing for joy: for thou shalt judge the people righteously, and govern the nations upon earth. Selah.
earth, and secure thine everlasting inheritance upon the same, that when it shall be glorious, thou shalt be glorious also.
¶ When the Lord thy God shall cut off the nations from before thee, whither thou goest to possess them, and thou succeedest them, and dwellest in their land;
Before the Lord; for he cometh to judge the earth: with righteousness shall he judge the world, and the people with equity.
All nations whom thou hast made shall come and worship before thee, O Lord; and shall glorify thy name.
